items.text:
TODAY, GHANA CLEANS. ALL OF US. 🇬🇭🧹

It's National Cleaning Day in seven flood-hit regions — and today the public joins in.

President Mahama has directed every government appointee — ministers, MPs, MMDCEs — to leave their offices and personally lead the cleanup in their communities. Drains are being desilted, roads cleared, markets and lorry parks scrubbed.

The theme says it all: "Our Actions, Our Future: Cleaning Ghana after the Floods."

Two weeks ago, floods took at least 13 lives in Accra. Today is about making sure water flows where it should.

(Source: GhanaWeb | Citi Newsroom | Graphic Online)
#GreaterNews #Ghana #NationalCleaningDay #AccraFloods

Are you cleaning up today? Send us photos from your community! 📸

Today Ghana cleans: National Cleaning Day in 7 flood-hit regions, with the public joining security agencies and MMDAs. Mahama has directed ministers, MPs and MMDCEs to personally lead cleanups. (Source: GhanaWeb/Citi) #GreaterNews #Ghana #NationalCleaningDay

THE NEW BLACK STARS COACH WILL BE NAMED BY MONDAY. 🇬🇭⚽

Sports Minister Kofi Adams has confirmed it: Ghana will know its new head coach by Monday.

One name is reportedly OUT: Hervé Renard. Reports say the two-time AFCON winner turned Ghana down because of his contract with Saudi Arabia.

So who takes the job? The GFA has stayed tight-lipped — but with AFCON qualifiers coming, the decision can't wait.

(Source: GBC | MyJoyOnline | GHANAsoccernet | Foot Africa)
#GreaterNews #Ghana #BlackStars #GFA

Who do you think gets it — and who SHOULD get it? 👇

New Black Stars coach to be named by Monday, Sports Minister Kofi Adams confirms. Reports: Hervé Renard is out — his Saudi contract keeps him. GFA decision due before AFCON qualifiers. (Source: GBC/MyJoyOnline) #GreaterNews #BlackStars

AFRICA'S FASTEST-GROWING EBOLA OUTBREAK HAS NOW KILLED 600 PEOPLE.

Congo's Ebola outbreak has reached 600 deaths and 1,759 confirmed cases — and health officials say it is spreading beyond the epicenter.

Suspected cases have now appeared in Tshopo and Haut-Uele provinces, including the city of Kisangani. A WHO official warns the outbreak is "spreading largely undetected" — four in five new cases have no known link to existing patients.

This strain — Bundibugyo — has no approved vaccine, though treatment trials began last week. The Africa CDC calls it the fastest-growing Ebola outbreak the continent has seen.

(Source: NPR | PBS | Euronews | US News)
#GreaterNews #Ebola #Congo #Africa #WorldNews

Congo's Ebola outbreak: 600 dead, 1,759 confirmed cases — and spreading beyond Ituri to Tshopo and Haut-Uele. WHO official: it's moving "largely undetected." Africa CDC calls it the continent's fastest-growing. (Source: NPR/PBS) #GreaterNews #Ebola #Africa
